1.3 Activity: Exploring Story Change Through Conflict
1. Write a short scene (500 words max) where a character must make a decision.
• The scene should start in one emotional state and end in another (hopeful → crushed, confident → uncertain, etc.).
2. Identify the key antagonist in this scene.
• Is it a person? Their own self-doubt? The environment?
3. Now rewrite the scene with a different antagonist.
• What happens if instead of a mentor, the pressure comes from a parent?
• How does this alter the tension and character choices?

📝 Purpose:
This teaches you how to make conflict dynamic and layered, rather than just one-dimensional.

SECTION 2: Studying Your Inspirations

Before crafting your own story, let’s analyze your key inspirations to understand why they work and how their structures can guide your own.

2.1 Your Inspirations: The Narrative DNA of Your Story
